_source: Google Calendar API v3 events.list (calendar.events.list) documented response shapes — https://developers.google.com/calendar/api/v3/reference/events/list. The googleapis Node SDK returns the wire body under `response.data`; `data.items[]` are calendar_v3.Schema$Event objects. Field names + shapes match the real wire: `summary` (title), `start`/`end` as {dateTime, timeZone} (timed) or {date} (all-day, exclusive end), `htmlLink`, `hangoutLink`, `conferenceData.entryPoints[].uri`, `attendees[].{email,displayName,responseStatus,self,organizer,optional}`, `organizer.{email,displayName,self}`, `iCalUID`, `recurringEventId`, `created`, `updated`. Captured via GET https://www.googleapis.com/calendar/v3/calendars/{calendarId}/events?singleEvents=true&orderBy=startTime.
_why: Replayed through GoogleCalendarClient.listEvents (which runs the genuine mapEvent normalizer in @elizaos/plugin-google) and then through lifeOpsCalendarEventFromGoogle so the full raw events.list wire -> GoogleCalendarEvent -> LifeOpsCalendarEvent chain is validated against the real Google Calendar shape, with no network (the GoogleApiClientFactory is faked). google-calendar-connector.real.test.ts re-fetches the live API with a token to detect drift from this recording.
